sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_data (id INT PRIMARY KEY, encoded_string VARCHAR(255), numbers INT);
INSERT INTO test_data (id, encoded_string, numbers) VALUES (1, split_and_decode('A=1&B=2', 1), 100);
INSERT INTO test_data (id, encoded_string, numbers) VALUES (2, split_and_decode('X=10&Y=20&C=30', 0), NULL);
SELECT id, encoded_string FROM test_data WHERE numbers IS NOT NULL AND split_and_decode(encoded_string, 1) IS NOT NULL;
SELECT id, split_and_decode(encoded_string, 0) AS decoded FROM test_data WHERE split_and_decode(encoded_string, 1) AND numbers < 150;
SELECT id, COUNT(*) FROM test_data GROUP BY id HAVING split_and_decode(encoded_string, 1) IS NOT NULL AND COUNT(*) > 1;
SELECT id, split_and_decode(encoded_string, 0) AS decoded FROM test_data WHERE encoded_string IS NOT NULL AND split_and_decode(encoded_string, 1) AND numbers >= 100;
SELECT id FROM test_data a JOIN (SELECT id, split_and_decode(encoded_string, 1) AS sub_decoded FROM test_data) b ON a.id = b.id WHERE a.numbers AND b.sub_decoded IS NOT NULL;
SELECT id, split_and_decode(encoded_string, 1) AS result FROM test_data WHERE split_and_decode(encoded_string, 0) AND numbers = 100 OR result IS NULL;
ALTER TABLE test_data ADD COLUMN new_column VARCHAR(100);
UPDATE test_data SET new_column = split_and_decode('P=10&Q=20', 1) WHERE id = 1 AND split_and_decode(encoded_string, 1) IS NOT NULL;
